title: Anatomy of train/serve skew
description: The three skew sources — schema, distribution, and computation — and how each manifests between offline and online.
body: Use when reasoning about why a model underperforms online despite strong offline metrics. Train/serve skew has three sources: schema skew (a feature present offline but missing/renamed online), distribution skew (training data sampled differently than live), and computation skew (the same feature computed by different code paths). Do not assume one cause — diagnose which applies. Actions: compare offline and online schemas, compare distributions, and replay an entity to compare computed values. Validation signals include a per-source skew check and a replay log diffing offline vs online features. Risk boundary: untreated skew silently caps production accuracy; if computation skew is found, unify the definition before re-evaluating.
